_meta.description: PrinterXPL-Forge CVE catalog — printers, MFPs, scanners, fax, Windows print spooler. Generated by André Henrique (@mrhenrike) | União Geek
_meta.author: Andre Henrique (@mrhenrike) | Uniao Geek

cves.description: HP JetDirect path traversal via HTTP allows unauthenticated attacker to write arbitrary files. Exploited by writing shell script to /etc/profile.d/ then triggering SNMP reboot for root RCE.

cves.versions_affected: multiple enterprise models prior to 2025 patch
cves.description: Unauthenticated buffer overflow triggered by malformed XPS print job submission via web interface. Allows arbitrary code execution as root on the printer firmware.
HP LaserJet Enterprise MFPs expose physical ports that allow attackers to extract sensitive data from the device without authentication.
Stack-based buffer overflow in HP LaserJet Enterprise MFP web server allows unauthenticated RCE via specially crafted HTTP request.
HP JetDirect printers leak the hex-encoded EWS JetAdmin password via specific SNMP OID GET requests, granting unauthenticated admin access to the Embedded Web Server.
HP LaserJet PJL allows directory traversal via the FSDIRLIST and FSUPLOAD commands enabling unauthenticated read of arbitrary files from printer filesystem.
Buffer overflow in PostScript interpreter of Canon imageRUNNER series allows unauthenticated RCE via crafted PS print job sent to raw port 9100.
Buffer overflow in PCL interpreter of Canon imageRUNNER series allows unauthenticated RCE via crafted PCL print job.
Canon TR150 printer driver allows low-privileged users to write malicious DLLs to the CanonBJ directory, which PrintIsolationHost.exe loads as SYSTEM during printer installation.
Canon wireless printers allow remote DoS via HTTP request to /English/pages_MacUS/cgi_lan.cgi. Device requires physical power cycle to recover.

cves.versions_affected: firmware prior to 2023 patch across 100+ models
cves.description: Server-side request forgery in Lexmark Web Services allows unauthenticated attackers to achieve RCE. Affects 100+ Lexmark printer models. Demonstrated at Pwn2Own Toronto 2022.
Unauthenticated command injection in Lexmark httpd via /cgi-bin/fax_change_faxtrace_settings when no admin user is set. bash eval injection allows arbitrary command execution as root.
Unauthenticated credential dump from Lexmark httpd -- LDAP credentials, local credentials exposed via the fax diagnostics endpoint.

cves.versions_affected: affected firmware with downgrade protection bypass
cves.description: Lexmark firmware update mechanism allows downgrade to older vulnerable firmware versions by bypassing version checks, re-exposing previously patched vulnerabilities.

cves.versions_affected: Ricoh Windows driver with RICOH_DRV path
cves.description: Low-privileged users can write malicious DLLs to the RICOH_DRV directory. Race window when PrintIsolationHost.exe loads printer drivers during installation allows escalation to SYSTEM.

Authenticated OS command injection in Xerox VersaLink web interface allows an attacker with admin credentials to execute arbitrary commands as root. Can chain with credential pass-back for full unauthenticated exploitation.
Xerox VersaLink LDAP configuration allows attacker with admin access to redirect LDAP authentication to attacker-controlled server, capturing domain credentials in cleartext.
Xerox VersaLink SMB/UNC scan-to-folder configuration allows attacker to redirect SMB authentication to attacker-controlled server, capturing NTLM hashes for offline cracking.
OS command injection in Sharp MFP web interface allows unauthenticated remote code execution as root via specially crafted HTTP requests.
Brother printers leak device serial numbers unauthenticated via HTTP, SNMP, PJL, or IPP. Serial number is used as seed for default admin password derivation (CVE-2024-51978).

cves.product: Multiple Brother / Konica Minolta / Toshiba TEC printers
cves.description: Default admin password for Brother printers is deterministically derived from the device serial number (exposed via CVE-2024-51977). An attacker can compute the password without any prior knowledge and gain full admin access.
Kyocera MFPs expose a SOAP service on port 9091 that allows unauthenticated export of the device address book, containing cleartext LDAP, SMB, email, and other stored credentials.
Epson printer firmware vulnerability allowing authenticated remote code execution via the web management interface.
Epson printer firmware authentication bypass allows unauthenticated access to admin functions and configuration interfaces.

cves.versions_affected: cups-browsed <= 2.0.1 on all Linux distros with CUPS
cves.description: cups-browsed binds to UDP 631 on INADDR_ANY and accepts DNS-SD printer announcements without validation. Attacker broadcasts a malicious IPP server URI; cups-browsed fetches and installs a malicious PPD file. Any subsequent print job triggers FoomaticRIPCommandLine RCE as the lp user.
cfGetPrinterAttributes5 in libcupsfilters does not sanitize IPP attributes returned from a printer server, allowing injection of malicious content into PPD files used in the CUPS RCE chain.
ppdCreatePPDFromIPP2 in libppd writes attacker-controlled data into a PPD file without sanitization, enabling injection of FoomaticRIPCommandLine directives for OS command execution.
foomatic-rip in cups-filters allows arbitrary command execution via FoomaticRIPCommandLine directive in PPD files, completing the CUPS RCE chain when a print job is triggered.

PrintNightmare: Windows Print Spooler RpcAddPrinterDriver allows low-privileged or remote authenticated users to install a malicious printer driver DLL executed as SYSTEM. Enables both remote RCE and local privilege escalation.

PrintNightmare (full exploit): Extends CVE-2021-1675. Windows Print Spooler allows remote authenticated or local low-privileged users to execute arbitrary code as SYSTEM via AddPrinterDriverEx(). Affected all Windows versions including DC-level systems.

SpoolFool: Low-privileged users can create directories under SpoolDirectory due to improper ACL on C:\Windows\System32\spool\printers, then use directory junctions to redirect DLL loading, achieving SYSTEM-level code execution.

GooseEgg: Exploited by APT28 (Fancy Bear) in the wild. Windows Print Spooler elevation of privilege via JavaScript constraint file manipulation. Listed in CISA KEV. Used for credential theft and lateral movement in nation-state attacks.

PrintDemon: Windows Print Spooler and Fax service allow low-privileged users to write arbitrary content to arbitrary files via port monitor DLL loading, enabling SYSTEM-level code execution through persistent print port manipulation.

FAXPLOIT: Memory corruption in T.30 fax protocol parsing allows attacker to compromise an MFP by sending a malicious fax, requiring only access to the target's fax number. Enables network penetration from an air-gapped phone line. Affects HP OfficeJet Pro 8720 and Samsung MFPs.

FAXPLOIT companion: Stack-based buffer overflow in TIFF parsing during fax reception allows RCE via malicious fax. Works together with CVE-2018-5924 to achieve reliable code execution via telephone line.

cves.versions_affected: all printers with PJL enabled on port 9100
cves.description: PJL (Printer Job Language) commands allow unauthenticated read and write of printer NVRAM, environment variables, and filesystem. Enables persistent backdoor configuration, password reset, job capture, and display message manipulation.

cves.versions_affected: all printers with SNMP v1/v2c enabled
cves.description: SNMP community string brute-force reveals configuration. Printer-MIB write access with rw community allows factory reset via OID .1.3.6.1.2.1.43. PML (Printer Management Language) bypass possible via SNMP SET. Enables complete device takeover.

Remote code execution in Windows PostScript and PCL6 Class printer driver. Attacker sends malicious print job via SMB or IPP that exploits a parsing vulnerability in the driver, executing code in the context of the print service.

Canon PIXMA printers accept unsigned firmware updates via unprotected HTTP endpoint. An attacker on the local network can flash arbitrary firmware. The encryption algorithm for .ful firmware files has been reverse-engineered.

cves.versions_affected: all PS printers with startjob / operators accessible
cves.description: PostScript startjob operator can be redefined to capture all subsequent print jobs in plaintext. Allows complete interception of documents printed by all users on the device. Documented by PRET and PrinterXPL-Forge research.

cves.product: Multiple vendors (Brother, Konica, Samsung, HP, Lexmark, Dell)
cves.versions_affected: all printers accessible on LAN from browser
cves.description: Cross-Site Printing (XSP): Browser-based attack using hidden iframes to POST PJL/PostScript payloads directly to port 9100 on internal network printers via the victim's browser. No direct network access required from attacker. Implemented as BeEF module.

cves.product: MFP with LDAP authentication (HP, Xerox, Ricoh, Lexmark)
cves.versions_affected: all MFPs with configurable LDAP server
cves.description: MFPs with configurable LDAP authentication can be reconfigured to point to an attacker-controlled LDAP server. When users authenticate at the copier panel, credentials are sent in cleartext or as NTLMv2 hashes to the attacker server.

cves.versions_affected: MFPs with FTP or HTTP file system access enabled
cves.description: MFP internal storage (scan-to-file, print jobs, address books) accessible via unauthenticated or weak-auth FTP/HTTP. Historical scan/print/copy documents can be exfiltrated. Documented in multiple enterprise MFP audits.
Epson LPD (Line Printer Daemon) service accepts print jobs without authentication, allowing unauthenticated document printing and queue enumeration.

cves.versions_affected: all printers with IPP port 631 exposed
cves.description: Internet Printing Protocol allows unauthenticated enumeration of printer attributes, installed jobs, and submission of print jobs. Many printers expose IPP without authentication by default.

cves.versions_affected: all SMB-enabled printers or print servers
cves.description: SMB-connected printers can have print jobs intercepted via NBNS/LLMNR poisoning using Responder. Print jobs routed through attacker system, documents captured in plaintext before forwarding.

cves.product: Printers with configurable network scan destinations
cves.description: Printers with scan-to-URL or HTTP notification features can be used as SSRF pivots to reach internal network resources not otherwise accessible. Printer becomes a proxy for scanning internal services.

Epson Connect cloud printing assigns a unique email address per printer. Knowledge of the email allows anyone on the internet to print to the device without authentication. Email can be discovered via OSINT or SNMP.

PrintDemon bypass: corrects CVE-2020-1048 fix via hardlink from port monitor file to arbitrary path, re-enabling arbitrary privileged file write. Achieves persistent SYSTEM code execution via print port DLL loading.

Windows Print Spooler elevation of privilege via improper privilege management when loading printer driver DLL. Allows local users to escalate to SYSTEM. Part of the PrintNightmare family of vulnerabilities.

Windows Print Spooler Remote Code Execution: low-privileged user can trigger print of a remote shared printer, causing the spooler to load an attacker-controlled driver DLL as SYSTEM. Does not require auth if printer sharing is enabled.

Windows Print Spooler elevation of privilege via NULL pointer dereference in handling of printer jobs. Allows local users to cause the service to crash (DoS) or execute arbitrary code as SYSTEM with race-condition exploitation.

Windows Print Spooler hardlink elevation of privilege. Low-privileged user creates a hardlink from printer spool file to a sensitive system file, enabling SYSTEM-level overwrites. Part of the cluster of 2022 spooler LPEs.

Windows Print Spooler elevation of privilege via improper file handling. Allows local attackers to write arbitrary data to privileged file locations, achieving SYSTEM code execution. Patched in July 2022.
Windows Print Spooler elevation of privilege. Allows low-privileged local attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges via improper handling of printer driver operations. Patched January 2023 Patch Tuesday.
Windows Print Spooler elevation of privilege via improper write access. Allows local attacker to write arbitrary data to privileged locations, achieving SYSTEM code execution. Patched June 2023.

Authenticated OS command injection in Xerox WorkCentre 78xx web interface. The clone_group parameter on the cloning page (/cgi-bin/cloning) passes user input directly to the C system() function without sanitization, allowing arbitrary OS command execution as 'nobody' web server user. Discovered by Compass Security (CSNC-2021-002) during a red team engagement.

cves.versions_affected: latest firmware versions (at time of disclosure 2021)
cves.description: Stack-based buffer overflow in Ricoh SP printer wpa_supplicant_conf_parser function. The /etc/wpa_supplicant.conf first line length > 0x38 bytes triggers a stack overflow via strncpy. Attacker controlling the WiFi config file can cause DoS or achieve RCE with carefully crafted overflow data. Discovered by Ainevsia.

Pre-authentication stack-based buffer overflow in Canon imageCLASS MFP SLP (Service Location Protocol) implementation. The vulnerability exists in SLP request parsing: user-supplied data length is not validated before copying to a fixed stack buffer (CWE-121). Unauthenticated remote attacker can send a crafted UDP packet to port 427 to execute arbitrary code as root. Discovered by ZDI (ZDI-CAN-15845).

Lexmark printers have incorrect access control (CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function). Unauthenticated attackers can access critical device functions without credentials, including configuration changes and firmware operations.

cves.product: MP C3004/C4504/C6004 and related Aficio series
cves.description: Command injection in Ricoh MP series web administration panel. Authenticated attacker (or via default credentials) can inject OS commands through device configuration parameters, achieving remote code execution on the embedded Linux system.

cves.product: HP Enterprise LaserJet / HP LaserJet Managed (50+ models, FutureSmart 5.6)
cves.versions_affected: FutureSmart 5.6 prior to 5.6.0.1 (M455) / 5.6.0.2 (others), with IPsec enabled
cves.description: Critical information disclosure in HP Enterprise LaserJet FutureSmart 5.6 firmware when IPsec is enabled. Scan job data (including credentials for scan-to-email, scan-to-SharePoint destinations) can be exposed to network attackers. Only affects the short exposure window of FS 5.6 with IPsec active. HP advisory HPSBPI03838.

cves.versions_affected: firmware prior to CXTPC.230.210 and other model-specific patches (April 2024)
cves.description: Heap-based buffer overflow in Lexmark Internet Printing Protocol (IPP) implementation (CWE-122). Unauthenticated adjacent network attacker can craft a malformed IPP request causing heap overflow, enabling arbitrary code execution on the device. Discovered via Trend Micro Zero Day Initiative (ZDI-CAN-22549) by Interrupt Labs.

Server-side request forgery (SSRF) in Lexmark embedded web server. Authenticated or unauthenticated attackers can abuse scan/notification destination configuration to make the printer issue HTTP requests to arbitrary internal network hosts, using the printer as an SSRF pivot.

cves.versions_affected: HP Enterprise firmware prior to security patch 2023-Q4
cves.description: Authentication bypass vulnerability in HP LaserJet Enterprise firmware management interface. Unauthenticated attacker can bypass authentication checks on the firmware update endpoint and upload arbitrary firmware, enabling persistent compromise, rootkit installation, or device bricking.

cves.product: Ricoh MP series / EWS (Embedded Web Server)
cves.versions_affected: Ricoh MP firmware prior to 2024 patch
cves.description: Remote code execution in Ricoh multifunction printers via malformed HTTP request to the Embedded Web Server. Unauthenticated attacker can send a specially crafted HTTP packet to trigger memory corruption in the web server, leading to arbitrary code execution on the device firmware.

Authentication bypass in Toshiba e-STUDIO multifunction printer web management interface (TopAccess). Unauthenticated attacker can bypass login checks to gain full admin access, enabling configuration changes, credential harvesting, firmware manipulation, and print job interception.

cves.versions_affected: CUPS 2.4.16 with shared PostScript queue enabled
cves.description: Unauthenticated RCE in CUPS 2.4.16 via shared PostScript queue. CUPS accepts anonymous print jobs by default and does not sanitize newline characters in print options. Attacker injects newline into a print option, which bypasses option escaping and is treated as a 'PPD:' trusted control record, allowing modification of printer queue PPD to execute arbitrary commands as 'lp'. Discovered by AI agents (Asim Viladi Oglu Manizada, April 2026).

Local privilege escalation to root in CUPS 2.4.16. Unprivileged user creates a fake local printer on a specific port, exploiting a race condition before CUPS removes a symlink, achieving arbitrary root file overwrite. Chains with CVE-2026-34980 for full unauthenticated remote-to-root exploit chain.

cves.product: Ricoh Aficio MP C series (MP C2003/C3003/C4503/C6003 and related)
cves.versions_affected: all models with firmware update via HTTP without signature verification
cves.description: Ricoh Aficio MP series accepts unsigned firmware updates via HTTP POST to /cgi-bin/fw_update without cryptographic signature verification. Attacker on the local network can flash arbitrary modified firmware, enabling persistent root access, backdoors, data exfiltration, or physical damage. Research documented at BlackHat 2017 by Deral Heiland (Printer Hacking: For Fun and Profit).

Konica Minolta bizhub printers expose a firmware download endpoint /wcd/fwdl.cgi that accepts multipart firmware uploads without cryptographic signature verification. Network attacker can upload malicious firmware package to achieve persistent code execution at firmware level, enabling complete device compromise.

cves.versions_affected: models with HTTP firmware endpoint prior to mitigation
cves.description: Brother MFC/DCP series printers accept firmware updates via HTTP PUT to /firmware endpoint without cryptographic signature verification. An attacker with admin access (achievable via CVE-2024-51978 default password derivation) can upload a malicious .blf firmware file, achieving persistent code execution at firmware level.

cves.versions_affected: models with EWS firmware update without signature check
cves.description: Epson WorkForce printers allow unauthenticated firmware updates via the Embedded Web Server. Modified firmware can disable security controls, exfiltrate print/scan data, or brick the device. Also documented: Epson Connect cloud exposure where printer email addresses can be used to send print jobs from the internet.

HP LaserJet 400 M401 web admin interface command injection. Authenticated attacker (or with default credentials) can inject OS commands through device configuration fields, enabling arbitrary command execution on the embedded firmware.

Canon imageRUNNER remote code execution via LAN Fax module. Malformed LAN Fax configuration request triggers buffer overflow in embedded Canon firmware, enabling unauthenticated remote code execution as root on the device.

cves.versions_affected: WF series EWS prior to fix
cves.description: Epson WorkForce printer series Embedded Web Server remote code execution. Malformed HTTP POST to EWS endpoint allows arbitrary command execution on embedded Linux. Publicly released on ExploitDB.

cves.versions_affected: DCP-1510 web admin prior to fix
cves.description: Brother DCP-1510 web administration interface remote code execution. Authentication bypass and command injection allow unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the printer's embedded OS.

cves.versions_affected: all PJL printers with filesystem access
cves.description: Metasploit auxiliary module that uses PJL FSUPLOAD to download any file from printer filesystem to local machine. Primarily used to extract stored documents, configuration files, credentials, and print/scan job data from MFP storage.

Metasploit HP PJL scanner: enumerates printer model, firmware version, environment variables, and available filesystems via PJL INFO commands. Used for reconnaissance and to identify candidates for further exploitation.

Metasploit exploit module for CVE-2020-1048 PrintDemon. Leverages print spooler file write vulnerability to install a persistent SYSTEM-level DLL backdoor that cannot be removed because the spooler service restarts automatically. Creates a permanent elevated backdoor on the target Windows system.

cves.versions_affected: Multiple JetDirect firmware versions prior to 2012
cves.description: PJL INFO VARIABLES command discloses the PJL admin password and web interface password in cleartext without any authentication. Affects virtually all HP LaserJet and OfficeJet Pro devices with JetDirect firmware.

Dell B-series laser printers (Lexmark OEM) expose 11 BlackHat 2017 attack vectors: SNMP write-enabled factory reset (PML MIB), PJL INFO VARIABLES credential disclosure, blank web admin password, anonymous IPP, FTP filesystem access, PS infinite loop DoS, NVRAM wear. No authentication required for most attacks.

OKI B-series laser printers expose multiple configuration pages (network config, user list, SNMP communities) without authentication via HTTP. Default credentials (admin/aaaaaa) often unchanged. BlackHat 2017 matrix: 8 vulnerabilities including PS infinite loop, PJL credential disclosure, NVRAM damage.

Samsung network printers expose an unauthenticated SNMP OID that returns device serial number, model, and admin credentials. Combined with default web password (sec00000), allows full unauthenticated access to web admin interface and print job manipulation.

cves.product: Any WSD-enabled MFP (Epson, HP, Xerox, Canon, Kyocera)
cves.description: Web Services on Devices (WSD) protocol exposes detailed device metadata (model, serial, firmware, capabilities) via unauthenticated UDP probes (port 3702) and SOAP GET metadata (port 5357). Enables fingerprinting of all printers/MFPs on the local network segment for subsequent targeted attacks.

cves.product: HP, Xerox, Ricoh, Kyocera, Brother, Sharp MFPs with fax
cves.description: MFPs with fax functionality expose FAX address books, speed-dial lists, email-forward rules, and fax transmission logs via unauthenticated HTTP endpoints or SNMP MIBs. Exfiltration of corporate fax addresses, confidential recipients, and scanned document routing rules.

cves.product: Any IPP-capable printer/MFP (HP, Canon, Epson, Brother, Kyocera)
cves.versions_affected: All printers accepting anonymous IPP Purge-Jobs
cves.description: Printers that accept unauthenticated IPP Purge-Jobs requests (RFC 2911, operation 0x0012) allow any network user to flush all pending print jobs. Affects HP LaserJet, Epson EcoTank, Brother MFC, Kyocera ECOSYS, Canon imageRUNNER and many others. Equivalent to a queue denial-of-service.

cves.versions_affected: All printers with IPP anonymous job submission
cves.description: Many network printers accept IPP Print-Job requests from any user without authentication. Enables arbitrary document injection, banner printing, resource exhaustion (paper/toner), and social engineering via printed content. Affects Epson EcoTank, HP InkJet/LaserJet, Brother MFC, and Canon PIXMA series.

cves.product: HP JetDirect, Ricoh, Xerox, Konica Minolta, Brother MFPs
cves.versions_affected: All printers with embedded FTP server
cves.description: Network printers and MFPs with embedded FTP servers (port 21) often use blank or weak default credentials (admin/blank, admin/access). Authenticated FTP access exposes the printer filesystem: scanned documents, config files, email addresses, LDAP credentials, and password files.

cves.versions_affected: All networks using mDNS-based IPP printer discovery
cves.description: An attacker on the local network can advertise a rogue IPP printer via mDNS/DNS-SD, causing clients to send print jobs to the attacker's machine. All documents (PDFs, Word docs, PostScript) are captured in plaintext. Credentials embedded in print jobs (Basic Auth, Kerberos tickets) may also be captured.
Network BOF in HP printer rendering subsystem. Unauthenticated RCE.

cves.versions_affected: Multiple HP Workpath models prior to 2023 patch
cves.description: BOF in HP Workpath MFP platform affecting 1914 models. Unauthenticated RCE.

cves.versions_affected: Multiple LaserJet models prior to 2024 patch
cves.description: BOF in PDF font rendering path. Unauthenticated RCE via crafted print job.

cves.product: HP LaserJet (380+ models), Samsung CLP/CLX/ML, Xerox B2xx/PH30xx/WC
cves.versions_affected: SSPORT.SYS driver — all versions since 2005 prior to 2021 patch
cves.description: Windows kernel driver SSPORT.SYS buffer overflow via IOCTL → SYSTEM LPE.
Stored XSS via unauthenticated HTTP PUT to /DevMgmt/ProductConfigDyn.xml.
XPS XML namespace parser BOF. Unauthenticated RCE. Part of Canon advisory CP2026-001.
XPS glyph run attribute BOF. Unauthenticated RCE. Part of Canon advisory CP2026-001.

cves.product: Multiple Lexmark MFP models (CX93x, MX91x etc.)
cves.description: Stack BOF in Lexmark PS interpreter via crafted PostScript document. Unauthenticated RCE.

cves.product: Multiple Lexmark models (MX911, MX912, XM8150 etc.)
cves.description: Memory corruption in Lexmark PS composite font parser. Unauthenticated RCE.
Heap BOF in Lexmark HTTP parser via oversized multipart content-disposition filename.
Heap BOF in getCFFNames() function. Pwn2Own Vancouver 2026 (ANHTUD). Unauthenticated RCE.

cves.product: Multiple Ricoh MFPs + Konica Minolta OEM variants
cves.description: Stack BOF in Ricoh Web Image Monitor HTTP server via crafted request header.
Unauthenticated heap BOF in Xerox IPP service via oversized printer-uri attribute.
Stack overflow in Xerox HTTP server Content-Type header parsing. Unauthenticated RCE.
Unauthenticated OS command injection in configrui.php via unsanitized param field.

cves.product: Brother MFC/DCP/HL (748+ models) + Konica Minolta bizhub OEMs
cves.description: WSD forced TCP connections — printer initiates TCP to attacker-controlled host (network scan).
Malformed WSD message causes Brother device crash and reboot (DoS).
Brother web admin allows LDAP/SMTP server override → credential pass-back attack.
OS command injection in PM43 web UI username field via newline injection.

cves.product: Brother MFC/DCP printers + multi-vendor embedded TFTP servers
cves.description: Infinite loop in embedded TFTP server triggered by crafted error packet → DoS.
HP LaserJet EWS Server-Side Request Forgery. CVSS 8.6 HIGH.
HP LaserJet MFP UART diagnostic buffer overflow. CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL.
HP PageWide Enterprise solution bundle unsigned upload RCE. CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL.
HP Web JetAdmin 10.3 unauthenticated RCE. CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL.
Xerox AltaLink unauthenticated EIP admin API. CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL.
CUPS cups-browsed UAF Stage 1 Pwn2Own 2026. CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL.
CUPS heap spray Stage 2 Pwn2Own 2026. CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL.
CUPS job-name stack overflow Stage 3 Pwn2Own 2026. CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL.
CUPS Pwn2Own 2026 full 4-stage chain orchestrator. CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL.
ZeroLogon via print server domain account. CVSS 10.0 CRITICAL.
